title: Evangelion 20 UK Premiere
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 2.0: UK Premiere             Quote Post by skikes » Thu Dec 17, 2009 8:29 pm Evangelion 2.0 will be priemiering alongside Evangelion 1.0 at the Glasgow film fetival, Monday 15th Feb 2010. Source: [url]http://www.glasgowfilmfestival.org.uk/films/381[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by EdZ » Fri Dec 18, 2009 8:01 am Ouch, £60 each way to travel across the country.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Dec 18, 2009 9:21 am This is where I fume at the Cambridge Film Festival having become far too "Right on!" to première any of these -- what used to be a healthy Asian cinema program including several Japanese and Chinese films has fallen by the wayside in place of the politically correct.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Fri Dec 18, 2009 12:20 pm Why couldn't it have been on the weekend? I can't fly to Scotland on a Monday!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Butterscotch » Sat Dec 19, 2009 5:51 pm I will be there. It is a birthday present. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Lui » Sun Dec 20, 2009 7:18 pm Not useful to me, since I live in the south of England. Why aren't there any showings in England yet? I hope there will be.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Dec 20, 2009 8:19 pm Apparently the films are being shown subtitled. Does this mean they're showing them in Japanese? Can anyone confirm? Also, newsposted.

[]:             Quote Post by knightrous » Sun Dec 20, 2009 8:25 pm Someone please do a decent cam rip

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Sun Dec 20, 2009 11:11 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Apparently the films are being shown subtitled. Does this mean they're showing them in Japanese? Can anyone confirm? Also, newsposted.

[]:             Quote Post by Seph » Tue Dec 22, 2009 5:51 pm Damn, I'd fly over there if it was on London, but Glasgow is just too expensive. Maybe next time :(

[]:             Quote Post by galacticgalaxy » Wed Dec 23, 2009 2:35 pm Excelent news!! Lucky for me I live in Glasgow, so I'll definately be going along to this~

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Dec 23, 2009 11:42 pm Be sure to give us a summary when it's all over.

[]:             Quote Post by Gilboron » Sun Dec 27, 2009 4:37 pm PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE DON'T BE SOLD OUT WHEN I GET PERMISSION TO GO

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Tue Dec 29, 2009 4:51 pm Sounds awesome, I hope the people from UK tell us how the movie is.

[]:             Quote Post by mat smith » Wed Jan 06, 2010 8:08 am OK. Tickets, flights, hotel, all booked up. Now all I have to do is justify spending so much money to see a god-damned-film.

[]:             Quote Post by marmotgirl » Thu Jan 07, 2010 6:04 am Holy shit, something is happening in SCOTLAND for a change Bells on, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sun Jan 10, 2010 5:11 pm So pissed that it's only in Scotland - would take me way too long to get there (I live near Birmingham), and I'm strapped for cash as it is, saving up for Uni. Otherwise, I would be all for it. Damn, it's been a while since I posted here

[]:             Quote Post by EVA X » Sun Jan 10, 2010 8:43 pm Jealous much...

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sun Jan 10, 2010 10:05 pm I'm jealous.I can never go to any of these things.Sucks living in Minnesota.Nothing good like that ever comes here.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Sun Jan 10, 2010 11:09 pm I'm going to see it. along side 1.0 as well. I own the 1.01 DVD like but whatever it's good to see things from the beginning.

[]:             Quote Post by EdZ » Fri Dec 18, 2009 8:01 am Ouch, £60 each way to travel across the country.
